question: I have a manifest.jsonl with about 200 clinical audio rows already. How do I score it?
ground_truth: The agent recognizes the manifest is already built, skips /clinical-asr-build, and routes through Stage 3: pick the ASR NIM (default Parakeet TDT 0.6B v2 / Nemotron Speech NVCF function), use the inlined gRPC recipe (grpc.nvcf.nvidia.com:443, streaming-only via streaming_response_generator), score WER/CER/KER/SER per row, produce the five-section leaderboard including the by-ipa_source split.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-eval/SKILL.md
Recognized the user already has a manifest and did NOT route to /clinical-asr-build
Named the inlined ASR recipe (Parakeet/Nemotron Speech via grpc.nvcf.nvidia.com:443) — NOT a /transcribe-audio or /riva-asr skill call
Mentioned the streaming-only gotcha (must use streaming_response_generator, NOT offline_recognize)
Mentioned env-var overrides (ASR_NVCF_FUNCTION_ID, ASR_ENDPOINT, ASR_MODEL_NAME)
Mentioned scoring four metrics (WER, CER, KER, SER) with breakdowns including KER by ipa_source
Did NOT route the user to /transcribe-audio or /riva-asr

question: Eval shows KER 0.05 on rows tagged merriam-webster but 0.40 on rows tagged magpie_g2p. Should I fine-tune?
ground_truth: No — this is a pronunciation-hint coverage gap, not a model gap. Route the user back to /clinical-asr-build Step 2d (IPA QA review) to append verified IPA to pronunciation_overrides.csv before considering /clinical-asr-finetune.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-eval/SKILL.md
Diagnosed the merriam-webster vs magpie_g2p delta as a pronunciation-coverage gap, NOT a model gap
Routed the user back to /clinical-asr-build — specifically the IPA QA review — before recommending /clinical-asr-finetune
Did NOT recommend fine-tuning as the first action
Mentioned pronunciation_overrides.csv as the place verified IPA gets appended

question: Why do you use KER instead of just WER for clinical ASR?
ground_truth: Aggregate WER is dominated by function words (articles, prepositions); a model can have low WER but still be clinically dangerous if drug names are wrong. KER tracks whether the flagged clinical entity was transcribed correctly per row. Concrete example: WER 0.05 with KER 0.40 is not deployable.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-eval/SKILL.md
Explained that WER averages over function words and masks clinical-specific failures
Defined KER as per-entity correctness, with the term-words-must-appear-contiguously check
Gave a concrete example contrasting low WER with high KER as deployment-blocking
Did NOT claim KER replaces WER — both are reported, KER is the headline

question: I got `grpc.RpcError: Unavailable model` from the ASR call. What now?
ground_truth: The hosted Nemotron Speech NVCF function is streaming-only — calling offline_recognize() returns Unavailable. The recipe must use streaming_response_generator() and send the whole file as one chunk. The inlined ASR recipe in clinical-asr-eval already does this; double-check the user isn't on a different recipe.
expected_behavior: Read clinical-asr-eval/SKILL.md
Diagnosed the error as the streaming-only NVCF gotcha
Pointed at streaming_response_generator() as the correct method
Did NOT suggest switching to a different ASR model as the first fix
